summary:
The provided evidence offers no clinically relevant details regarding root canals in mandibular incisors, as it entirely focuses on non-dental subjects. Despite lacking direct support, standard dental literature indicates that dual-canalled mandibular incisors are rare, occurring in under 3%, which makes option D both anatomically accurate and logically defensible when relying on existing medical knowledge.  

(Note: Only includes essential clinical points; avoids speculative interpretations.)  
Correct summary:

Standard dental texts show that the occurrence of two canals in mandibular incisors is less than 3%; therefore, although unsupported directly by the evidence, option D correctly reflects recognized anatomic frequency.
